author: PC Gamer
text:
Lies of P studio is hiring an 'AI Creator' for future projects but says the role will not be 'directly involved' in the development of the sequel
The successful applicant will use AI tools like Midjourney and Stable Diffusion for "asset creation," post-processing, and training AI models for use in game library creation.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sega has canceled its live service 'Super Game' due to 'intensifying market competition,' and I really, really hope it's a sign that the industry is finally correcting itself
Years of catastrophic bets on F2P mega hits may finally be subsiding.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After his shock retirement last year, legendary former Tekken boss has landed at one of Japan's biggest fighting game publishers
Katsuhiro Harada is now the CEO of VS Studio.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve snuck a Wilhelm scream Easter egg into the new Steam Controller
My Steam Controller just screamed at me, Wilhelmingly.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Elon Musk, Sam Altman and the world's billionaires are terrified of the Google AI genius behind a 25-year-old computer game, because they think he might actually end up controlling god
"There is a very low probability of a good future if someone doesn't slow Demis down."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Gang of Dragon studio website disappears without notice as NetEase funding dries up
Nagoshi Studio, founded in 2022 by Yakuza creator Toshihiro Nagoshi, just revealed its debut project at the 2025 Game Awards.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Path of Exile's overwhelming complexity isn't the real reason new players pass on it, director believes: 'I think that PoE 1's being such an old game is in itself the accessibility problem'
PoE 2 is certainly complicated, but it's much kinder to new players.

author: PC Gamer
text:
One of the best rated games on Steam this week is an action platformer where you can parry a giant industrial digger with a chainsaw
It is, admittedly, a pretty good pitch.

author: PC Gamer
text:
20 years later, the world deserves another Sid Meier's Railroads!
Just three years after Railroads! came out, we were already reappraising it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve made more than 27 million unique images for Counter-Strike listings as part of its new 'major update' to Steam's Community Market
The big marketplace update is live now in beta.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dark Souls 2 path tracing mod is now available for all, and yes, it inevitably makes the game harder
Finally, a reason to use Prism Stones.

author: PC Gamer
text:

The successful applicant will use AI tools like Midjourney and Stable Diffusion for "asset creation," post-processing, and training AI models for use in game library creation.

text:

text:

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve made more than 27 million unique images for Counter-Strike listings as part of its new 'major update' to Steam's Community Market
The big marketplace update is live now in beta.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Protesters 'raid' EA to oppose Saudi Arabia acquisition
A small group of people from the Players Alliance HQ attempted to deliver a petition against the takeover containing more than 70,000 signatures.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This vampire farm sim has Real Housewives of Stardew energy
Moonlight Peaks is brave enough to ask: What if Stardew Valley were more toxic?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Former NetEase exec's new investment group is funding Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic because 'everyone wins' if you 'give the best creator the opportunity to work on a dream project'
After raising $100 million, GreaterThan Group is also funding a shooter from former Black Ops design director David Vonderhaar.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Singleplayer simulated MMO Erenshor is getting raids this summer
You'll be able to join 14 computer-controlled players in an endgame loot grind.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to find the Serial Number for the Probe in Directive 8020
Get straight to sending your signal.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sony assures that AI is only meant to 'augment' artists' capabilities instead of replacing them but it feels like a slippery slope
And that's not including upscaling and PSSR.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New testing shows DisplayPort 2.1 is very picky about the monitor cable you're using
If you want the full 80 Gbps of bandwidth, you'll need a DP80-certified cable.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We're doing something a bit more interesting': All Will Rise dev says its progressive deck-builder shouldn't be dumped in the 'woke, liberal bucket'
A river god has been murdered, and it's up to you to hold the rich and powerful to account.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Popular PlayStation emulator clamps down on AI submissions: 'Leave behind something useful to humanity when you're gone, instead of peddling slop'
'As for all the AI bros seething on our socials, we're simply blocking you.'

author: PC Gamer
text:
Wordle is becoming a primetime TV quiz show, because the ultimate five-letter word is 'money'
The show will be presented by Savannah Guthrie and co-produced by Jimmy Fallon.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We're starting to get convincing counterfeit DDR5 modules, just in case the memory crisis isn't bad enough already
A different kind of memory crisis.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've spent 30 years with Microsoft but Linux and Pop! OS might have converted me
Linux has saved me from the grubby claws of Windows hell, but it comes with a catch.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mixtape is at the center of another tedious culture war discourse, and I think I know why
Nostalgia's a powerful thing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to find the correct chemical agents for Anders in Directive 8020
Bypass the puzzle of finding the correct chemical agents in Episode 5.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Lip-Bu Tan says 'Intel and Nvidia are collaborating to develop exciting new products' and I'm wondering what they could possibly be
Presumably involves a lot of AI, but I hope not.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find the key code to Carter's PC in Directive 8020
Uncover the secret to Carter's PC in Episode 2.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Sinking City 2 shifts the series to survival horror, and manages to be genuinely unsettling
The first game was atmospheric, but not really scary. Here's hoping the sequel gets it right.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Today I learned that Gears of War creator Cliff Bleszinski produced a Tony Award-winning musical
CliffyB contains multitudes.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's Big Battlemage Arc Pro B70 GPU benchmarked in games, ends up bang-on Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti 16 GB performance
So much silicon for such modest performance.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dark Souls 2 path tracing mod is now available for all, and yes, it inevitably makes the game harder
Finally, a reason to use Prism Stones.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Lies of P studio is hiring an 'AI Creator' for future projects but says the role will not be 'directly involved' in the development of the sequel
The successful applicant will use AI tools like Midjourney and Stable Diffusion for "asset creation," post-processing, and training AI models for use in game library creation.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sega has canceled its live service 'Super Game' due to 'intensifying market competition,' and I really, really hope it's a sign that the industry is finally correcting itself
Years of catastrophic bets on F2P mega hits may finally be subsiding.

author: PC Gamer
text:
True 3rd-person view has been hidden in a single Metal Gear Solid 2 variable for 15 years—now we're finally getting it
Which probably stings for that guy who modded it into the original game from scratch.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Framework says DDR5 memory costs 'remain fairly stable' but 'on SSDs, the pricing story is unfortunately not as positive' as it raises prices again
One step forward, two steps back.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dark Souls 2 path tracing mod is now available for all, and yes, it inevitably makes the game harder
Finally, a reason to use Prism Stones.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This city-smashing monster sim is the first game I've ever played where my reward for beating a level is getting a nuke dropped on my head
You Are the Monster is a simple, scrappy chunk of kaiju chaos.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Here's how the Iran war is hitting key supplies for components, according to supply chain experts
The ripples span wider than you might think.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Taking a heroic stand against the holy trinity, 5 bears beat a WoW dungeon on its highest difficulty—Blizzard says oops, quickly reverts 200% buff that caused it
Unbear-able

author: PC Gamer
text:
Teaching software company strikes a deal with hackers to get customer data back, defying FBI guidance
A strange choice with details to follow.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ken Levine says Steam Machine shows we're hitting 'diminishing returns' with bleeding-edge graphics tech—just look at BioShock to see why art is more important
Keep your traced rays, I'm attaching barnacles to things.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Outbound is an excellent idea but fails to pin down what people love about co-op crafting
My van's wheels are stuck on a single track.

author: PC Gamer
text:
One of BioShock's directors has been questioning the point of one of its key systems: 'I don't care about how many different plasmids I have'
Imsim legend Jonathan Chey's new game is exploring a solution to plasmid favouritism.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bioshock lead's fears about Judas were banished by Baldur's Gate 3 gibbing a main character on his second playthrough: 'He was gone'
"I don't have a heartbreak about people not seeing player content."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Subnautica 2 release date and launch times
It's nearly time to cannonball into a new underwater survival adventure.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Immersive sim sickos rejoice: the makers of Void Bastards are creating their most choice-driven game to date in Godzone 6
Build a grotesque mutant and then play to their hideous strengths in a highly-detailed sci-fi dungeon.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We would need to shut off power for half the country': Microsoft's $1 billion Kenya data center project runs out of juice
'Future generations will be overburdened by the current decisions.'

author: PC Gamer
text:
True 3rd-person view has been hidden in a single Metal Gear Solid 2 variable for 15 years—now we're finally getting it
Which probably stings for that guy who modded it into the original game from scratch.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Framework says DDR5 memory costs 'remain fairly stable' but 'on SSDs, the pricing story is unfortunately not as positive' as it raises prices again
One step forward, two steps back.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cooler Master MasterFrame 360 Panorama review
A great way to show off your Hatsune Miku statue.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Konami belatedly realises hey, we might have something here, as the first properly new Silent Hill in 13 years sells over 2 million
Well done everyone.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition review
It's the most powerful gaming CPU money can buy, but you really shouldn't.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Trump confirms he brought Jensen Huang on his expedition to convince China to 'open up'
Or as Trump calls him, 'Great Jensen Huang'.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Talos Principle 3 promises an 'epic grand finale' to the tale of humanity's rebirth
The end is coming soon.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'What if behind the pointer, there was an AI model': Google DeepMind wants to reinvent the humble mouse cursor
It was not broke, and I don't think they fixed it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
You could win a $500 Amazon gift card by playing Subnautica 2 this month, so remember to send us your best video clips!
Play Subnautica 2. Record yourself. Send us your vids. Win a prize.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Bioshock series has some of gaming's best intros because lead dev Ken Levine knows 'if you have a mediocre beginning, you're done'
First impressions are everything.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Germany cuts Steam Deck desktop devs a $1.5 million cheque, they declare an end to 'insecure spyware-riddled software imposed by the likes of Microsoft'
Linux. Maybe you've heard of it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
There's a devious hacking scheme that involves a hijacked Microsoft Teams account, a fake IT helpdesk, and a covert infection tool
IT's a pretty bad one.

text:

You Are the Monster is a simple, scrappy chunk of kaiju chaos.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AI is 'creatively soulless,' says director of Mass Effect and Knights of the Old Republic: 'I’m just really unimpressed with it'
It doesn't sounds like AI is going to play much of a role in the development of Fate of the Old Republic.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Embark says the 'pressure of a monthly cycle' is holding Arc Raiders back, so it's slowing down: Major updates will come every 6 months from now on
The Frozen Trails update planned for October will add the largest Arc Raiders map to date.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The guy who literally wrote the textbook on AI says when it comes to the extinction scenario the human race is cooked: 'Making these systems more capable… doesn't seem like a sensible move'
"The CEO of Google DeepMind expressed very similar concerns: that they were in a race that they couldn't pull out of."

author: PC Gamer
text:
The 'numbers just aren't there,' Dead Space producer says, and that's why we'll probably never see Dead Space 4
Dead Space has dedicated fans, but not enough of them to matter.

text:

A great way to show off your Hatsune Miku statue.
